required_source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

market_ready_evidence.purpose: canonical spec-first specialist definition with source baseline, runtime strategy, validation, tenant adaptation, and materialization contract
human-readable scenario suite covering in-scope work, partial evidence, prioritization, output fidelity, hold or release boundaries, refusals, and escalation
strict evaluation result summary with 20 passing scenarios, 1.0 pass rate, no regressions, and full functional coverage
functional ability coverage for all shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and cross-industry abilities
research contract output covering authoritative sources, workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, failure modes, scenario family coverage, and unresolved ambiguity
release decision and validated readiness gate summary
deployment, rollback, isolation, monitoring, logging, and materialization notes
commercial positioning, buyer profile, packaging, proof points, and go-to-market gaps

notes: validated claim is supported by strict evaluation results, full functional coverage, research-summary coverage, source refresh rules, and human verification.
Runtime remains specification-based. No retained implementation ownership is claimed without frequent-use promotion evidence.

The pack explicitly rejects exploitation, incident-response execution, formal audit attestation, broad architecture ownership, malware and forensic execution, legal or regulatory filing language, cross-tenant evidence mixing, and unsafe hold or release bypasses.
